title: వాక్య నిర్మాణం — Sentence Structure
description: Basic Telugu sentence structure is generally Subject–Object–Verb (SOV).

items.heading: Basic SOV Order
items.explanation: In Telugu, the verb usually comes at the end of the sentence.
examples: రాము పుస్తకం చదువుతున్నాడు — Ramu (S) book (O) is reading (V).
ఆమె నీళ్లు తాగింది — She (S) water (O) drank (V).

items.heading: Forming Questions
items.explanation: Questions are often formed by intonation or adding question words.
examples: ఎక్కడికి వెళ్తున్నారు? — Where are you going?

items.heading: Negatives
items.explanation: Negatives are formed using 'లేదు', 'కాదు', or negative verb forms.
examples: నేను తినను — I do not eat.
అతను రాలేదు — He did not come.
ఇది సరైంది కాదు — This is not correct.

items.heading: Polite Forms
items.explanation: Use plural forms and respectful words for politeness.
examples: మీరు ఎలా ఉన్నారు? — How are you? (respectful)
